title: HITL Sensor Emulation Platform
subtitle: Hardware-in-the-loop board for avionics sensor and actuator validation
role: Electrical design, schematic architecture, PCB layout, validation planning
stackup: 8-layer mixed-signal PCB with separated analog/digital regions
summary: A board-level HITL platform for validating sensor acquisition modules by emulating RTDs, current-loop pressure transducers, thermocouples, valves, and differential sensors.
mainConstraints: Low-noise analog emulation near high-current actuator loads
Deterministic command and measurement paths for HITL automation
Clear separation between digital control, analog front ends, and valve interfaces
Accessible test points for bring-up and validation
publicNote: Public portfolio version omits proprietary harness details and final requirement limits.

tests.name: Power Rail Bring-up
tests.purpose: Verify all generated rails before loading sensitive ICs.
tests.setup: Bench supply current-limited at input, measure 5 V, 3.3 V, analog references.
tests.expected: Rails within tolerance, no abnormal current draw.
tests.notes: Initial test should be performed before installing high-value analog ICs if the assembly process allows it.

tests.name: RTD Emulation Accuracy
tests.purpose: Validate emulated resistance/temperature behavior against expected ADC readings.
tests.setup: Command DAC values and compare ADC output against expected PT100 curve.
tests.expected: Error within defined tolerance across operating range.
tests.notes: Capture error tables and note calibration assumptions in the wiki.

tests.name: Current Loop Load Sweep
tests.purpose: Confirm pressure-transducer emulation across expected loop loading.
tests.setup: Sweep command values while logging loop current and rail temperature.
tests.expected: Loop current tracks command without thermal or compliance failures.
tests.notes: Use external electronic load for boundary cases.
